messages.content: Describe an API design tradeoff you made.

For approval workflows, we kept the public REST API coarse-grained so product teams could create an approval request in one call, while internal services used smaller events for audit and retry handling.
How did you improve backend reliability?
I added idempotency keys to billing webhook processing, wrote pytest coverage around duplicate delivery, and added retry logging so support could see whether a failure was transient.

human_notes: Validates API design and reliability ownership in a non-coding interview.
